1	\chapter{The tautological quotient and the universal property of $\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)$}
2	\label{chap:Picard_GrassmannianQuot}
3	
4	\providecommand{\ShMod}{\mathrm{SheafOfModules}}
5	
6	The previous chapter constructed the Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over
7	\(\mathbb{Z}\) by gluing the affine charts \(U^I\)
8	(\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) along the transition isomorphisms
9	(\cref{def:gr_transition}) satisfying the cocycle condition
10	(\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}), and proved that the structure morphism
11	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is separated (\cref{lem:gr_separated})
12	and proper (\cref{lem:gr_proper}). This chapter adds the next layer: the
13	\emph{tautological rank-\(d\) quotient}
14	\(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of the
15	trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf, and the \emph{universal property} that makes
16	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) the fine moduli space of rank-\(d\) locally free quotients of
17	\(\mathcal{O}^{r}\) -- equivalently, the representing scheme of the Quot functor
18	of the trivial sheaf.
19	
20	Throughout, \(r \ge d \ge 1\) are the fixed integers of the previous chapter, and
21	for a size-\(d\) subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) we write \(R^I\) for the
22	chart ring \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) of \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) and \(R^I_J\) for the
23	localisation along \(P^I_J = \det(X^I_J)\) defining the overlap
24	\(U^I_J \subseteq U^I\).
25	
26	\section{Infrastructure: locally free sheaves and module gluing}
27	\label{sec:grquot_infra}
28	
29	The constructions of this chapter -- the universal quotient sheaf
30	\(\mathcal{U}\), the tautological quotient \(u\), and the functor of points -- rest
31	on two pieces of foundational machinery for sheaves of modules. The first is the
32	predicate ``locally free of rank \(d\)'' (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}),
33	which records that a sheaf of modules is, on the members of some open cover,
34	isomorphic to the trivial bundle \(\mathcal{O}^{d}\); it is the condition cut out
35	in the definition of the Grassmannian functor. That predicate is the project-local
36	\(\mathrm{SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank}\) already set up in the Quot-scheme
37	chapter (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}), which this chapter reuses verbatim;
38	the only foundational machinery genuinely new to this chapter is the second piece:
39	a gluing primitive that descends a sheaf of modules (and a morphism of such
40	sheaves) from per-chart data along an open cover, given a transition cocycle
41	compatible with the cover's own gluing; it is the vehicle that assembles the
42	per-chart free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}^{d}_{U^I}\) and the per-chart quotients
43	\(u^I\) into the global \(\mathcal{U}\) and \(u\), and is set up below.
44	
45	Stating the triple-overlap multiplicativity of the descent datum, however, first
46	requires a small piece of base-change machinery for the pullback functor on sheaves
47	of modules: the three transition isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts live a
48	priori on three different overlaps, and to compare their composite they must be
49	transported to a single common overlap. The next two blocks supply exactly this
50	transport -- the pseudofunctorial comparison of iterated pullbacks
51	(\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}) and its packaging along the triple-overlap data of a
52	scheme glue datum (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) -- after which the
53	cocycle condition can be expressed cleanly.
54	
55	\begin{definition}[Comparison of iterated pullbacks of sheaves of modules]
56	  \label{def:modules_pullbackComp}
57	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}
58	  \mathlibok
59	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
60	  Let \(a : X \to Y\) and \(b : Y \to Z\) be morphisms of schemes. For a sheaf of
61	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-modules \(\mathcal{M}\), pulling back first along \(b\) and then
62	  along \(a\) agrees with pulling back along the composite \(b \circ a\): there is a
63	  natural isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
64	  \[
65	    a^{*}\bigl(b^{*}\mathcal{M}\bigr)
66	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
67	    (b \circ a)^{*}\mathcal{M},
68	  \]
69	  natural in \(\mathcal{M}\), expressing that \(\mathcal{M} \mapsto \mathcal{M}\)'s
70	  pullback is a pseudofunctor on schemes. These comparison isomorphisms satisfy the
71	  pentagon (associativity) coherence for a triple of composable morphisms. This is the
72	  pullback half of the pseudofunctoriality of sheaves of modules and is supplied by
73	  Mathlib.
74	\end{definition}
75	
76	\begin{lemma}\leanok
77	[Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap]
78	  \label{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}
79	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport}
80	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp, def:gr_the_glue_data}
81	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) with charts
82	  \(U_i\), overlaps \(V_{ij}\), overlap immersions \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow
83	  U_i\), transitions \(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\), and triple-overlap
84	  data \(t'_{ijk}\) with its compatibility identity \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\). For an index
85	  triple \((i,j,k)\) write
86	  \[
87	    V_{ijk} \;=\; V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}
88	  \]
89	  for the common triple overlap, with the two projections
90	  \(p^{ij}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ij}\) and \(p^{ik}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ik}\), and
91	  let \(t'_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{jki}\) be the glue datum's triple
92	  transition, satisfying
93	  \[
94	    t'_{ijk} \,\circ\, p^{jk}_{jki} \;=\; p^{ij}_{ijk} \,\circ\, t_{ij}
95	    \qquad (t_{\mathrm{fac}}).
96	  \]
97	  Given per-chart sheaves of modules \(\mathcal{M}_i\) and a transition isomorphism
98	  \[
99	    g_{ij} \;:\; f_{ij}^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
100	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
101	    (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j
102	  \]
103	  on the overlap \(V_{ij}\) (as in \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}), the
104	  \emph{base-change transport} of \(g_{ij}\) to the triple overlap \(V_{ijk}\) is the
105	  isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{ijk}}\)-modules
106	  \[
107	    \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}
108	      \;:\;
109	    (f_{ij} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
110	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
111	    (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j
112	  \]
113	  obtained by pulling \(g_{ij}\) back along \(p^{ij}_{ijk}\) and reassociating the
114	  iterated pullbacks via \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. With the analogous transports
115	  \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) (the first taken along
116	  \(t'_{ijk}\), so that \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) identifies its source and target sheaves
117	  with those of \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\)), all three
118	  transported isomorphisms become endomorphism-composable: \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\)
119	  and \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) land on a common sheaf, and their composite is
120	  comparable to \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) on \(V_{ijk}\).
121	\end{lemma}
122	
123	\begin{proof}
124	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp, def:gr_the_glue_data}
125	  The pullback functor on sheaves of modules is a pseudofunctor: for composable
126	  scheme morphisms it carries the comparison isomorphisms of
127	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}, natural in the sheaf and coherent for triple
128	  composites. Pulling the transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\) back along the projection
129	  \(p^{ij}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ij}\) yields an isomorphism between
130	  \((p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*} f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) and \((p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}(t_{ij} \circ
131	  f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\); applying \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} to each side
132	  reassociates these into the single-pullback form
133	  \((f_{ij} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) and
134	  \((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\), giving
135	  \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\). For the \((j,k)\)-transition the same construction is
136	  applied along the triple transition \(t'_{ijk}\) rather than a bare projection; the
137	  glue datum's compatibility identity \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\),
138	  \(t'_{ijk} \circ p^{jk}_{jki} = p^{ij}_{ijk} \circ t_{ij}\), guarantees that the
139	  source of \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) coincides with the target of
140	  \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) (both are the pullback of \(\mathcal{M}_j\) along the same
141	  morphism \(V_{ijk} \to U_j\)), so the two are composable as endomorphism-style
142	  isomorphisms over \(V_{ijk}\). The reassociations introduced by
143	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} are coherent (they satisfy the pentagon identity), so
144	  the composite \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i} \circ \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) and the third
145	  transport \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) are isomorphisms between the same pair of
146	  sheaves on \(V_{ijk}\), and may be compared directly. This is the module-level
147	  analogue of the scheme-level triangle \(t'_{ijk}\)/\(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) and is the
148	  shape in which the multiplicative cocycle condition is stated below.
149	\end{proof}
150	
151	\begin{lemma}\leanok
152	[Triple-overlap endpoint bridges]
153	  \label{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
154	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src,
155	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid,
156	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt}
157	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data}
158	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) and fix
159	  indices \(i,j,k\) with triple overlap \(V_{ijk} = V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}\).
160	  The three transports of \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} have
161	  endpoints pinned down by three scheme-morphism equalities on \(V_{ijk}\), each a
162	  pure consequence of the glue datum's structural identities:
163	  \begin{itemize}
164	    \item (\emph{src}) \(\mathrm{fst} \circ f_{ij} = \mathrm{snd} \circ f_{ik}\)
165	      -- the defining pullback condition of \(V_{ijk}\);
166	    \item (\emph{mid}) \(\mathrm{fst} \circ (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})
167	      = (t'_{ijk} \circ \mathrm{fst}) \circ f_{jk}\) -- the pullback condition
168	      composed with the glue datum's \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) identity;
169	    \item (\emph{tgt}) \((t'_{ijk} \circ \mathrm{fst}) \circ (t_{jk} \circ f_{kj})
170	      = \mathrm{snd} \circ (t_{ik} \circ f_{ki})\) -- the cocycle core, from the
171	      \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) associativity, the reversed pullback condition, the
172	      \(t_{\mathrm{inv}}\) collapse and the glue datum's cocycle identity.
173	  \end{itemize}
174	  These three equalities align the source, middle, and target sheaves of the three
175	  transports so that the cocycle equation (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}
176	  typechecks.
177	\end{lemma}
178	\begin{proof}\leanok
179	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data}
180	  Each equality is an identity of morphisms in the category of schemes, proved by
181	  rewriting with the glue datum's axioms: \emph{src} is literally
182	  \(\mathrm{pullback.condition}\); \emph{mid} adds the \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)
183	  factorisation \(D.t_{\mathrm{fac}}\,i\,j\,k\); \emph{tgt} chains
184	  \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)-associativity at \((j,k,i)\), the reversed pullback
185	  condition, an auxiliary collapse combining \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)-associativity at
186	  \((k,i,j)\) with \(t_{\mathrm{inv}}\), and finally the cocycle identity
187	  \(D.\mathrm{cocycle}\,i\,j\,k\). No sheaf-of-modules data enters; these are
188	  bookkeeping equalities of the underlying scheme cover.
189	\end{proof}
190	
191	% HISTORICAL (abandoned route): an earlier draft built the glued sheaf as a hand-rolled
192	% presheaf of compatible families (decls gluePresheaf / gluePresheafModule / gluePresheafIsSheaf).
193	% That route is abandoned; the gluing is now the equalizer of pushforwards described in the
194	% Construction paragraph of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, which subsumes it.
195	
196	\begin{definition}
197	
198	\leanok
199	[Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover]
200	  \label{def:scheme_modules_glue}
201	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue}
202	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:modules_pullbackComp,
203	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
204	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}), with index set \(J\), charts \(U_i\),
205	  pairwise overlaps \(V_{ij}\), open immersions
206	  \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow U_i\), and transition isomorphisms
207	  \(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\) satisfying the glue datum's
208	  cocycle conditions; write \(X\) for the glued scheme and
209	  \(\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\) for the canonical open immersions of the
210	  charts. Suppose given:
211	  \begin{itemize}
212	    \item for each \(i \in J\), a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules \(\mathcal{M}_i\) on the chart \(U_i\);
213	    \item for each pair \((i,j)\), a \emph{transition isomorphism} of sheaves of
214	      modules on the overlap \(V_{ij}\),
215	      \[
216	        g_{ij} \;:\; f_{ij}^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
217	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
218	        (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j,
219	      \]
220	      comparing the two charts' sheaves after restriction (pullback) to the
221	      common overlap \(V_{ij}\);
222	  \end{itemize}
223	  \emph{subject to the following module cocycle conditions}, compatible with the
224	  scheme-level cocycle of \(D\) (its triple transition data \(t_{ij}\) and the
225	  overlap immersions \(f_{ij}\)), which are required as hypotheses on the family
226	  \((g_{ij})\):
227	  \begin{enumerate}
228	    \item[(C1)] \emph{self-identity:} the self-transition is the identity,
229	      \(g_{ii} = \mathrm{id}\) on \(f_{ii}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) (with
230	      \(V_{ii}\) and \(t_{ii}\) the diagonal data of \(D\));
231	    \item[(C2)] \emph{triple-overlap multiplicativity:} over each common triple
232	      overlap \(V_{ijk} = V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}\) the base-change transports
233	      (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three transition
234	      isomorphisms satisfy
235	      \[
236	        \widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i} \circ \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}
237	          \;=\; \widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j},
238	      \]
239	      where \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\), \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\),
240	      \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) are the transports of \(g_{ij}\), \(g_{jk}\),
241	      \(g_{ik}\) to \(V_{ijk}\) through the scheme-level triple transition
242	      \(t'_{ijk}\) and its compatibility \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\), reassociated by the
243	      pullback comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} so that all three land on
244	      the same sheaf -- the multiplicative, \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued cocycle
245	      condition.
246	  \end{enumerate}
247	  Conditions (C1) and (C2) are exactly the descent datum for the Zariski cover
248	  \(\{\iota_i\}\); it is subject to them that the gluing is well-defined. Out of this
249	  data the construction produces a glued sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
250	  \(\mathcal{M}\) on the glued scheme \(X\).
251	
252	  \emph{Construction.} The category \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of sheaves of
253	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has all limits, and both pushforward along a morphism and
254	  these limits preserve the sheaf condition; the glued sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is therefore
255	  built directly as a limit -- an \emph{equalizer of pushforwards} -- rather than through a
256	  hand-built presheaf of compatible families. Concretely, form the two parallel maps of
257	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
258	  \[
259	    \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
260	      \;\;\underset{b}{\overset{a}{\rightrightarrows}}\;\;
261	    \prod_{i,j} (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\,\bigl(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr),
262	  \]
263	  where, on the \((i,j)\)-component, leg \(a\) pushes the \(i\)-th factor forward along the
264	  overlap immersion \(f_{ij}\) using the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction
265	  together with the pushforward-composition comparison
266	  \((\iota_i)_{*} \cong (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\circ f_{ij}^{*}\) (the dual of
267	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}), and leg \(b\) pushes the \(j\)-th factor forward and
268	  additionally transports it across the inverse transition isomorphism \((g_{ij})^{-1}\)
269	  and the glue datum's compatibility identity, so that both legs land on the common overlap
270	  sheaf \((\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i)\). The glued sheaf is the
271	  equalizer
272	  \[
273	    \mathcal{M} \;=\; \operatorname{eq}(a,b) \;\hookrightarrow\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i,
274	  \]
275	  the sub-sheaf of tuples of pushed-forward chart sections whose two overlap restrictions
276	  agree -- precisely the compatible families, now realised as a categorical limit rather than
277	  a bespoke presheaf. The cocycle conditions (C1)/(C2) on the family \((g_{ij})\) are
278	  \emph{not} consumed in forming this equalizer object: the limit exists for any family of
279	  transition maps. They are instead what make the equalizer descend correctly, and are
280	  exactly the hypotheses pinned down downstream when the restriction isomorphisms
281	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso} are produced. This specialises effective descent
282	  of sheaves of modules to the concrete open cover \(\{\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\}\)
283	  supplied by a scheme glue datum, and is the form in which the Grassmannian's
284	  tautological bundle and quotient are built.
285	
286	  The glued sheaf comes with three further structures, each recorded as its own block
287	  downstream: the \emph{restriction isomorphisms} \(\rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}
288	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\) compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps
289	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}; the \emph{characterisation up to unique
290	  isomorphism} by the restriction property \cref{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}; and the
291	  \emph{descent of morphisms} -- a family \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
292	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms glues to a unique \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to
293	  \mathcal{N}\) with \(\rho_i^{\mathcal{N}} \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi = \phi_i \circ
294	  \rho_i^{\mathcal{M}}\) \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}.
295	\end{definition}
296	
297	\begin{definition}[Restriction isomorphisms of the glued sheaf]
298	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}
299	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso}
300	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
301	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
302	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
303	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. For each
304	  chart index \(i\) there is a \emph{restriction isomorphism} of sheaves of
305	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules
306	  \[
307	    \rho_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{M}_i,
308	  \]
309	  compatible with the transition isomorphisms: over each overlap \(V_{ij}\) the two
310	  restrictions \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\), transported to the common overlap, differ
311	  exactly by \(g_{ij}\).
312	\end{definition}
313	\begin{proof}
314	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
315	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
316	  By the equalizer-of-pushforwards description \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, a section of
317	  \(\mathcal{M}\) over an open of the form \(\iota_i^{-1}V\), restricted to the chart
318	  \(U_i\), is recovered from and recovers its \(i\)-th component
319	  \(s_i \in \Gamma(\mathcal{M}_i, \cdot)\) -- the \(i\)-th factor of the product
320	  \(\prod_i (\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) into which the equalizer embeds; the
321	  chart-restriction bridge \cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso} identifies the pullback
322	  \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\) with this restriction, and the \(i\)-th projection
323	  \((s_\bullet) \mapsto s_i\) is the isomorphism \(\rho_i\) (with inverse extending a chart
324	  section by the overlap relation).
325	  The overlap compatibility is the defining relation of the compatible families: over
326	  \(V_{ij}\) the components \(s_i, s_j\) are related by \(g_{ij}\), which is precisely the
327	  statement that \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\) differ by \(g_{ij}\) after transport to
328	  \(V_{ij}\); the triple-overlap coherence needed for this to be consistent is (C2),
329	  whose transports are aligned by \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} and
330	  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}.
331	\end{proof}
332	
333	\begin{lemma}[Uniqueness of the glued sheaf up to canonical isomorphism]
334	  \label{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}
335	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue_unique}
336	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
337	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
338	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
339	  Suppose \(\mathcal{M}'\) is another sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules equipped with
340	  isomorphisms \(\rho'_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}' \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\)
341	  compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps. Then there is a unique isomorphism
342	  \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) intertwining the restriction
343	  isomorphisms, i.e.\ with \(\rho'_i \circ \iota_i^{*}(\cdot) = \rho_i\) for every \(i\).
344	\end{lemma}
345	\begin{proof}
346	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
347	  A morphism of sheaves of modules on \(X\) is determined by, and may be assembled from,
348	  its restrictions to the members of the open cover \(\{\iota_i\}\), agreeing on the
349	  overlaps -- the locality and gluing of sections
350	  (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom. The
351	  intertwining condition forces the restriction over \(U_i\) to be
352	  \((\rho'_i)^{-1} \circ \rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}'\);
353	  these local isomorphisms agree on each overlap \(V_{ij}\) because both
354	  \((\rho_i)\) and \((\rho'_i)\) are compatible with the \emph{same} transition family
355	  \((g_{ij})\) (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}). By locality they glue to a
356	  unique global isomorphism \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) with the
357	  required property; uniqueness is the separation half of the same gluing.
358	\end{proof}
359	
360	\begin{definition}[Descent of morphisms along the cover]
361	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueHom}
362	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueHom}
363	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
364	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
365	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
366	  Let \((\mathcal{M}_i, g_{ij})\) and \((\mathcal{N}_i, h_{ij})\) be two glued data
367	  over the same glue datum \(D\), with glued sheaves \(\mathcal{M}, \mathcal{N}\) and
368	  restriction isomorphisms \(\rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i, \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i\). Given a family
369	  of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-linear morphisms \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
370	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms on every overlap (i.e.\ \(h_{ij}\) intertwines
371	  the pullbacks of \(\phi_i\) and \(\phi_j\) over \(V_{ij}\)), there is a unique morphism
372	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) with
373	  \[
374	    \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi \;=\; \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i
375	    \qquad \text{for every } i.
376	  \]
377	\end{definition}
378	\begin{proof}
379	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
380	  On each chart the prescribed identity defines a local morphism
381	  \((\rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i)^{-1} \circ \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i :
382	  \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{N}\). The commutation of the \(\phi_i\)
383	  with the transition isomorphisms \((g_{ij}, h_{ij})\), together with the overlap
384	  compatibility of the \(\rho\)-families (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}), makes
385	  these local morphisms agree on every overlap \(V_{ij}\). By the locality and gluing of
386	  sections (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom they
387	  promote to a unique global morphism \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) restricting
388	  to each \(\phi_i\) through the \(\rho_i\) -- the descent of \(\mathrm{Hom}\) along the
389	  cover.
390	\end{proof}
391	
392	\subsection{Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary morphism}
393	\label{sec:grquot_pullback_free}
394	
395	The functor of points (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}) acts on morphisms by pullback, so
396	it needs the pullback of the trivial bundle to be again trivial --
397	\(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I} \cong \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,\oplus I}\) -- for an
398	\emph{arbitrary} scheme morphism \(\varphi\). Mathlib's pullback-of-free comparison applies
399	only when the underlying site functor \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi\) is \emph{final}; the
400	following three blocks remove that restriction for every morphism, and the resulting
401	pullback machinery preserves rank-\(d\) local freeness.
402	
403	\begin{lemma}\leanok
404	[The opens-pullback functor is final]
405	  \label{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
406	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final}
407	  For any morphism of schemes \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the inverse-image functor on opens
408	  \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi : \mathrm{Opens}(T) \to \mathrm{Opens}(T')\),
409	  \(U \mapsto \varphi^{-1}U\), is \emph{final}.
410	\end{lemma}
411	
412	\begin{proof}
413	  Finality means that for every open \(V \subseteq T'\) the structured-arrow category of
414	  opens \(U \subseteq T\) with \(V \le \varphi^{-1}U\) is connected. That category is
415	  nonempty and has the terminal object \(U = \top\) -- one always has
416	  \(V \le \varphi^{-1}\top = T'\), and every object admits a unique arrow to \(\top\) --
417	  and a category with a terminal object is connected.
418	\end{proof}
419	
420	\begin{lemma}\leanok
421	[Pullback of a free sheaf is free]
422	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
423	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso}
424	  \uses{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
425	  For any scheme morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\) and index type \(I\), there is a canonical
426	  isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{T'}\)-modules
427	  \[
428	    \varphi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I}\bigr)
429	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
430	    \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,\oplus I}.
431	  \]
432	\end{lemma}
433	
434	\begin{proof}
435	  \uses{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
436	  Mathlib's pullback-of-free comparison produces this isomorphism whenever the site functor
437	  \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi\) is final; that finality is \cref{lem:gr_opensMap_final},
438	  which holds for every morphism, so the comparison applies unconditionally.
439	\end{proof}
440	
441	\begin{lemma}
442	
443	\leanok
444	[Free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism]
445	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom}
446	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom}
447	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
448	  Let \(\varphi, \psi : T' \to T\) be morphisms with \(\varphi = \psi\) and \(I\) an index
449	  type. The two free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} along \(\varphi\) and
450	  \(\psi\) agree after transporting the differing pullback sources by the canonical
451	  \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\): the composite of that transport with
452	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\psi)\) equals \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi)\).
453	\end{lemma}
454	\begin{proof}
455	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
456	  Substituting the equality \(\varphi = \psi\) collapses the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) to the
457	  identity and the two comparisons coincide.
458	\end{proof}
459	
460	\begin{lemma}
461	
462	\leanok
463	[Iso-level free-pullback cancellation]
464	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso}
465	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso}
466	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
467	  For equal base morphisms \(\varphi = \psi : T' \to T\) and index type \(I\), the composite
468	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi) \circ \mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\psi)^{-1}\) is the
469	  \(\mathrm{eqToIso}\) transport between the (differing) pullback sources of the free sheaf
470	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I}\). Stated and proved generically in the base morphisms, so its
471	  use never forces evaluation of a concrete immersion; this is the load-bearing replacement
472	  for the cast chain in the self-identity \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}.
473	\end{lemma}
474	\begin{proof}
475	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
476	  Substituting \(\varphi = \psi\) turns the composite into
477	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi) \circ \mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi)^{-1} =
478	  \mathrm{id}\), which is the \(\mathrm{eqToIso}\) of the now-trivial source equality.
479	\end{proof}
480	
481	\begin{lemma}\leanok
482	[Pullback preserves rank-\(d\) local freeness]
483	  \label{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}
484	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}
485	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:modules_pullbackComp}
486	  Let \(\varphi : T' \to T\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{M}\) a sheaf of
487	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules locally free of rank \(d\)
488	  (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}). Then the pullback \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{M}\) is
489	  locally free of rank \(d\) on \(T'\).
490	\end{lemma}
491	
492	\begin{proof}
493	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:modules_pullbackComp}
494	  Choose an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(T\) trivialising \(\mathcal{M}\),
495	  \(\mathcal{M}|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\,d}\). The preimages
496	  \(\{\varphi^{-1}U_i\}\) cover \(T'\). On each member the factorisation
497	  \(\varphi \circ \iota_{\varphi^{-1}U_i} = (\varphi|_{U_i}) \circ \iota_{U_i}\) of the
498	  restricted morphism, together with the pseudofunctor comparison of iterated pullbacks
499	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}), identifies
500	  \((\varphi^{*}\mathcal{M})|_{\varphi^{-1}U_i}\) with
501	  \((\varphi|_{U_i})^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\,d}\); the latter is
502	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\varphi^{-1}U_i}^{\,d}\) by \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. Hence
503	  \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{M}\) is locally free of rank \(d\).
504	\end{proof}
505	
506	\section{The tautological quotient on the charts}
507	\label{sec:grquot_chart}
508	
509	\emph{Infrastructure: scalar endomorphisms.} Realising a matrix of regular
510	functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules has no off-the-shelf primitive
511	in Mathlib; it is assembled from two small project-local helpers that turn a global
512	function into a scalar endomorphism of the structure sheaf, after which the matrix
513	is reconstituted entry-by-entry over a finite biproduct.
514	
515	\begin{definition}\leanok
516	[Global section of the unit module]
517	  \label{def:gr_globalUnitSection}
518	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.globalUnitSection}
519	  \uses{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
520	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(a \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) a global section of
521	  the structure sheaf. Viewing \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as the unit module
522	  \(\mathbf{1} \in \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (\cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}), the
523	  section \(a\) determines a global section of \(\mathbf{1}\): on each open
524	  \(V \subseteq X\) take the restriction \(a|_V \in \Gamma(V, \mathcal{O}_X)\), the
525	  family \((a|_V)_V\) being compatible with the restriction maps because
526	  restriction is functorial. This is the section of the unit sheaf attached to a
527	  global function.
528	\end{definition}
529	
530	\begin{definition}\leanok
531	[Scalar endomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)]
532	  \label{def:gr_scalarEnd}
533	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd}
534	  \uses{def:gr_globalUnitSection, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
535	  For \(a \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the \emph{scalar endomorphism}
536	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) : \mathcal{O}_X \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is the
537	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear endomorphism of the unit module given by multiplication
538	  by \(a\). It is obtained from the global section of \cref{def:gr_globalUnitSection}
539	  under the canonical identification of endomorphisms of the unit module
540	  \(\mathbf{1}\) with its global sections, \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong
541	  \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\); concretely, on each open \(V\) it acts as
542	  multiplication by \(a|_V\). These scalar endomorphisms are the matrix-entry
543	  building blocks of the chart quotient.
544	\end{definition}
545	
546	\begin{lemma}\leanok
547	[Scalar endomorphism of the unit is the identity]
548	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}
549	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_one}
550	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
551	  The scalar endomorphism attached to the unit function \(1 \in
552	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) is the identity of the unit module:
553	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}\).
554	\end{lemma}
555	
556	\begin{proof}
557	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
558	  Multiplication by \(1\) is the identity on every section, so under the
559	  identification \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) the section \(1\)
560	  corresponds to \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}\).
561	\end{proof}
562	
563	\begin{lemma}\leanok
564	[Scalar endomorphism of the zero function is zero]
565	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
566	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_zero}
567	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
568	  The scalar endomorphism attached to the zero function \(0 \in
569	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) is the zero endomorphism:
570	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\).
571	\end{lemma}
572	
573	\begin{proof}
574	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
575	  Multiplication by \(0\) sends every section to \(0\), so the corresponding
576	  endomorphism of the unit module is the zero map.
577	\end{proof}
578	
579	\begin{lemma}\leanok
580	[Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication]
581	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}
582	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_comp}
583	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
584	  For \(a, b \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the scalar endomorphisms compose by
585	  multiplication: \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) \circ \mathrm{scalarEnd}(b) =
586	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a\,b)\).
587	\end{lemma}
588	\begin{proof}
589	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
590	  Under the identification \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) the
591	  scalar endomorphisms act as multiplication by \(a\) and by \(b\); their composite
592	  is multiplication by \(a\,b\), because the section ring is commutative and
593	  restriction maps are ring homomorphisms.
594	\end{proof}
595	
596	\begin{lemma}\leanok
597	[Scalar endomorphism is additive]
598	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
599	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_add}
600	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
601	  For \(a, b \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) one has \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a + b) =
602	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) + \mathrm{scalarEnd}(b)\).
603	\end{lemma}
604	\begin{proof}
605	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
606	  Multiplication by \(a + b\) equals multiplication by \(a\) plus multiplication by
607	  \(b\) on every section, since the restriction maps of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) are additive;
608	  under \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) this is the stated additivity.
609	\end{proof}
610	
611	\begin{lemma}
612	
613	\leanok
614	[Scalar endomorphism of a finite sum]
615	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_sum}
616	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_sum}
617	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
618	  For a finite index set \(s\) and a family \(f : \iota \to \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\),
619	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(\sum_{i \in s} f_i\bigr) = \sum_{i \in s} \mathrm{scalarEnd}(f_i)\).
620	\end{lemma}
621	\begin{proof}
622	  \uses{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
623	  Induction on the finite set \(s\): the empty case is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\)
624	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}) and the insertion step is additivity
625	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}).
626	\end{proof}
627	
628	\emph{Infrastructure: matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf.} To realise the
629	\(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transitions one must promote an invertible \(d \times d\)
630	matrix of global functions to an \emph{automorphism} of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf
631	\(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\), exactly as the chart quotient \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}
632	realises the universal matrix. The next blocks assemble this matrix-to-morphism
633	functor and record its two structural identities -- that it carries matrix
634	multiplication to composition and the identity matrix to the identity -- which are
635	the algebraic heart of the bundle cocycle.
636	
637	\begin{definition}\leanok
638	[Matrix endomorphism of the free sheaf]
639	  \label{def:gr_matrixEnd}
640	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd}
641	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
642	  Let \(S\) be a scheme, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
643	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) a matrix of
644	  global functions. The \emph{matrix endomorphism} \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the
645	  endomorphism of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) whose
646	  \((p,q)\)-component, under the presentation of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) as the
647	  finite biproduct of \(d\) copies of the unit module \(\mathbf{1}\), is the scalar
648	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{p,q})\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}). It is
649	  assembled from these components by the universal property of the rank-\(d\)
650	  biproduct, exactly as the chart quotient \(u^I\) is assembled
651	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). This is the substitute for the (non-existent)
652	  Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) endomorphism of a free sheaf'' primitive.
653	\end{definition}
654	
655	\begin{lemma}\leanok
656	[Matrix endomorphism carries multiplication to composition]
657	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
658	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_comp}
659	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
660	  For \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) the matrix
661	  endomorphisms compose according to the matrix product, with the order reversed by
662	  the contravariance of the component indexing:
663	  \[
664	    \mathrm{matrixEnd}(M) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)
665	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N \cdot M).
666	  \]
667	\end{lemma}
668	\begin{proof}
669	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
670	  The composition of two biproduct-assembled morphisms is again biproduct-assembled,
671	  with \((i,q)\)-component the sum over the middle index \(p\) of the composites of
672	  components, \(\sum_p \mathrm{component}_{i,p} \circ \mathrm{component}_{p,q}\) (the
673	  categorical matrix product over the biproduct). Each composite of scalar
674	  endomorphisms is the scalar endomorphism of the product,
675	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\circ\mathrm{scalarEnd}(b) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(ab)\)
676	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}), and a finite sum of scalar endomorphisms is the
677	  scalar endomorphism of the sum (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}); hence the \((i,q)\)-component
678	  of the composite is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(\sum_p M_{q,p} N_{p,i}\bigr) =
679	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl((N\cdot M)_{q,i}\bigr)\), which is precisely the
680	  \((i,q)\)-component of \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N\cdot M)\). The reversal of the factor
681	  order is the contravariance of the column/component indexing.
682	\end{proof}
683	
684	\begin{lemma}\leanok
685	[Matrix endomorphism of the identity is the identity]
686	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}
687	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_one}
688	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
689	  The matrix endomorphism of the identity matrix is the identity morphism of
690	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\): \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(1_{d\times d}) =
691	  \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}}\).
692	\end{lemma}
693	\begin{proof}
694	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
695	  The diagonal components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\)
696	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the off-diagonal components are
697	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the morphism with this
698	  component matrix is the identity of the biproduct.
699	\end{proof}
700	
701	\begin{definition}\leanok
702	[Free-sheaf automorphism of an invertible matrix]
703	  \label{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}
704	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso}
705	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}
706	  Let \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) be mutually
707	  inverse, \(M N = 1\) and \(N M = 1\). The \emph{matrix automorphism}
708	  \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(M, N)\) is the isomorphism of free sheaves
709	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) with forward map
710	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) and inverse map \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)\); the two
711	  composites are identities by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp} (turning the products
712	  \(NM\) and \(MN\) into the relevant compositions) and \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}.
713	  Its forward map is \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) by construction
714	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom}).
715	\end{definition}
716	
717	\begin{lemma}\leanok
718	[Forward map of the matrix automorphism]
719	  \label{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom}
720	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso_hom}
721	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEnd}
722	  The forward map of \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(M, N)\) is \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\).
723	\end{lemma}
724	\begin{proof}
725	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEnd}
726	  Immediate from the definition \cref{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}, whose forward field is
727	  literally \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\).
728	\end{proof}
729	
730	\begin{lemma}\leanok
731	[Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively]
732	  \label{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul}
733	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso_mul}
734	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
735	  Let \(A, A', B, B'\) be \(d \times d\) matrices of global functions on \(S\) with

input.description: Locate target lemma labels in blueprint

content:
1000	  Write all matrices over the common triple-overlap ring. The image matrix of the
1001	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) is \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), and its \(K\)-minor is
1002	  computed by restricting to the \(K\)-indexed columns; since taking a column
1003	  submatrix commutes with left multiplication (\cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}),
1004	  \[
1005	    X^J_K \;=\; \bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_K .
1006	  \]
1007	  Both \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\) have unit determinant
1008	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so this product of invertibles inverts
1009	  by \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\):
1010	  \[
1011	    (X^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J).
1012	  \]
1013	  Multiplying on the right by \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) and cancelling \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1\)
1014	  via the two-sided Cramer identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}, the
1015	  cancellation packaged in \cref{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}) gives
1016	  \((X^J_K)^{-1} (X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\). The agreement of the three image
1017	  matrices over the triple overlap that licenses these manipulations is the content of
1018	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}, the matrix form of the scheme-level cocycle
1019	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}.
1020	\end{proof}
1021	
1022	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1023	[Scalar endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1024	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}
1025	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_pullback}
1026	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1027	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(a \in \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S)\) a
1028	  global function, with comorphism on global sections
1029	  \(p^{\sharp} : \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S) \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\).
1030	  Write \(q : p^{*}\mathbf{1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathbf{1}\) for the unit-pullback comparison
1031	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index). Then the pullback of the scalar endomorphism
1032	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) is conjugate, through \(q\), to the scalar endomorphism of the
1033	  base-changed function:
1034	  \[
1035	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\bigr)
1036	      \;=\; q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(p^{\sharp} a\bigr) \,\circ\, q
1037	  \]
1038	  as endomorphisms of \(p^{*}\mathbf{1}\); equivalently
1039	  \(q \circ p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp} a) \circ q\). This is the
1040	  single-entry naturality atom underlying the matrix-level statement
1041	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}.
1042	\end{lemma}
1043	\begin{proof}
1044	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1045	  The scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is multiplication
1046	  by the global section \(a\) on the unit module \(\mathbf 1 = \mathcal{O}_S\). Pulling back
1047	  along \(p\) sends multiplication by \(a\) to multiplication by its image, and the comorphism
1048	  \(p^{\sharp}\) is precisely the action of pullback on global functions, so
1049	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a))\) is multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\). The
1050	  comparison \(q\) is an isomorphism of unit modules intertwining this multiplication on
1051	  \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\) with multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(\mathbf 1\); conjugating back
1052	  along \(q\) therefore yields the claimed identity. The verification is a single computation on
1053	  the unit module, where multiplication by a function commutes with the natural comparison
1054	  \(q\).
1055	\end{proof}
1056	
1057	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1058	[Matrix endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1059	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1060	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_pullback}
1061	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1062	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1063	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1064	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\). Let
1065	  \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T))\) be the entrywise
1066	  image of \(M\) under the comorphism \(p^{\sharp}\), and let
1067	  \(Q : p^{*}(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) be the free-pullback
1068	  comparison \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at the rank-\(d\) index set. Then the
1069	  pullback of the matrix endomorphism \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q\), to
1070	  the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1071	  \[
1072	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\bigr)
1073	      \;=\; Q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q .
1074	  \]
1075	\end{lemma}
1076	\begin{proof}
1077	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1078	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1079	  By \cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}, \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the biproduct-assembled endomorphism
1080	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} = \bigoplus_{k} \mathbf 1\) whose \((k,\ell)\)-component is
1081	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\). The pullback functor \(p^{*}\) is a left adjoint, hence
1082	  additive and biproduct-preserving; through the coproduct comparison underlying the
1083	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation
1084	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) and the assembled morphism to the
1085	  morphism reassembled from the pulled-back components, the reassociation of the iterated
1086	  pullbacks being \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the
1087	  pulled-back scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the
1088	  comparison-conjugate of \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp}M_{k,\ell}) =
1089	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the biproduct, the conjugating
1090	  one-element comparisons combine into the single free comparison \(Q\), giving
1091	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)) = Q^{-1}\circ\mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M)\circ Q\).
1092	\end{proof}

1102	  natural identification of the global sections of an affine scheme with its coordinate ring
1103	  -- the counit \(\Gamma(\Spec A, \mathcal O) \xrightarrow{\sim} A\) of the
1104	  \(\Gamma \dashv \Spec\) adjunction, and likewise for \(B\) -- the global-sections base-change
1105	  comorphism
1106	  \[
1107	    (\Spec\varphi)^{\sharp} : \Gamma(\Spec A, \mathcal O) \longrightarrow \Gamma(\Spec B, \mathcal O)
1108	  \]
1109	  is identified with \(\varphi\) itself; that is, the square relating
1110	  \((\Spec\varphi)^{\sharp}\) to \(\varphi\) through the two counit isomorphisms commutes.
1111	\end{lemma}
1112	\begin{proof}
1113	  This is the naturality of the counit isomorphism \(\Gamma \circ \Spec \cong \mathrm{id}\) of
1114	  the global-sections / spectrum adjunction: for any ring homomorphism \(\varphi\) the comorphism
1115	  of \(\Spec\varphi\) on global sections is, after transport through the natural counit
1116	  isomorphisms at \(A\) and \(B\), the homomorphism \(\varphi\). No Grassmannian-specific data
1117	  enters; the statement is the affine-scheme naturality square applied to a single ring map.
1118	\end{proof}

6	
7	This file adds, on top of the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` built in
8	`GrassmannianCells.lean`, the tautological rank-`d` quotient
9	`u : O^r ↠ U` and the universal property making `Gr(d,r)` the fine moduli
10	space of rank-`d` locally free quotients of `O^r`.
11	
12	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex` (Nitsure §1).
13	-/

21	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf
22	
23	To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
24	need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
25	endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
26	`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
27	inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.
28	
29	These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
30	sheaves" primitive. -/
31	
32	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
33	
34	/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
35	determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
36	Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
37	noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
38	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=
39	  PresheafOfModules.sectionsMk
40	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
41	    (by
42	      intro Y Z f
43	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
44	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
45	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
46	      congr 1)
47	
48	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
49	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
50	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
51	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
52	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
53	
54	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
55	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
56	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
57	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
58	  ext Y
59	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
60	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
61	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
62	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
63	  exact map_one _
64	
65	/-- `scalarEnd 0` is the zero endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for identifying
66	the off-diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
67	lemma scalarEnd_zero : scalarEnd (0 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 0 := by
68	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
69	  ext Y
70	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (0 : Γ(X, ⊤))
71	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val
72	          (0 : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).app Y
73	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
74	  refine (map_zero _).trans ?_
75	  rfl
76	
77	/-- The value of the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` on a section `x` over `Y` is
78	multiplication by the restriction of `a`: `(scalarEnd a)(x) = x · a|_Y`. Project-local
79	helper, the computational heart of the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities below. -/
80	lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
81	    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
82	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
83	  rfl
84	
85	/-- `scalarEnd c` corresponds to the global section `c` under `unitHomEquiv`. -/
86	lemma unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd (c : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
87	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd c) = globalUnitSection c := by
88	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
89	
90	/-- The scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` sends the unit section `1` to `a|_Y`. -/
91	lemma scalarEnd_val_app_one (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ) :
92	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
93	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
94	  exact one_smul _ _
95	
96	/-- Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication: `scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b =
97	scalarEnd (a * b)`. Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` multiplicativity. -/
98	lemma scalarEnd_comp (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
99	    scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b = scalarEnd (a * b) := by
100	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
101	  change SheafOfModules.sectionsMap (scalarEnd b)
102	        ((SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd a))
103	      = (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd (a * b))
104	  rw [unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd, unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd]
105	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
106	  change (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
107	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a * b)
108	  exact (RingHom.map_mul (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b).symm
109	
110	/-- `scalarEnd` is additive: `scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b`.
111	Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` matrix-multiplication identity. -/
112	lemma scalarEnd_add (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
113	    scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b := by
114	  conv_lhs => rw [scalarEnd]
115	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
116	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
117	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
118	      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
119	        + (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
120	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, scalarEnd_val_app_one]

880	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_map, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, e1,
881	      Matrix.submatrix_map, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
882	  rw [hXJI] at hcol
883	  exact hcol.symm
884	
885	/-! ### L3 transport: `scalarEnd`/`matrixEnd` naturality under pullback
886	
887	The substantive new infrastructure for (C2). The atom is `scalarEnd_pullback`: pulling back a
888	scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` along a scheme morphism `p` is, after the unit-pullback
889	comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the base-changed function
890	`p.appTop a`. Its proof transposes the naturality square under the pullback-pushforward
891	adjunction to a `unit`-level identity, which holds by naturality of the comorphism `p.c`. -/
892	
893	/-- The reduced (transposed) form of the scalar-naturality atom: on the unit sheaf,
894	multiplication by `a` followed by the comorphism `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` equals the
895	comorphism followed by the pushforward of multiplication by `p.appTop a`. Project-local
896	helper for `scalarEnd_pullback`. -/
897	lemma unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
898	    scalarEnd a ≫ SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
899	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
900	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a)) := by
901	  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).obj
902	    (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).unitHomEquiv.injective
903	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
904	  -- Both `.val Y` are nested applications (no morphism composite) up to defeq, since
905	  -- `unitHomEquiv (f ≫ p) = sectionsMap p (unitHomEquiv f)` and `sectionsMap`/`unitHomEquiv`
906	  -- are `rfl`/`sectionsMk`-defined; rewrite into that form via `change`.
907	  change (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)).val.app Y
908	        ((scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : S.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
909	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
910	            (scalarEnd ((Scheme.Hom.appTop p) a))).val.app Y
911	        ((SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)).val.app Y
912	          (1 : S.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
913	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply,
914	    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply, map_one]
915	  -- Goal: `φ.hom.app Y (a|_Y) = ((pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))).val.app Y 1`.
916	  -- RHS reduces (defeq, `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply'` + `scalarEnd_val_app_one`)
917	  -- to `T.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (p.appTop a)`; LHS rewrites to the same by
918	  -- naturality of the comorphism `(toRingCatSheafHom p).hom` at `(homOfLE le_top).op : op ⊤ ⟶ Y`.
919	  have hnat := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom
920	    ((Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p).hom.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : Y.unop ≤ ⊤)).op) a
921	  rw [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, CategoryTheory.comp_apply] at hnat
922	  rw [hnat]
923	  -- The RHS pushforward (`PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply'` is `rfl`) evaluates the
924	  -- scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd (p.appTop a)` at `1` over the preimage open; both sides are
925	  -- then `T.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (p.appTop a)` (the `homOfLE`s agree by
926	  -- proof irrelevance, the comorphism by `forget₂`-on-elements), so `scalarEnd_val_app_one` closes.
927	  exact (scalarEnd_val_app_one ((Scheme.Hom.appTop p) a)
928	    (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map p.base).obj (Opposite.unop Y)))).symm
929	
930	/-- **ATOM: scalar endomorphism naturality under pullback** (`lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback`).
931	For `p : T ⟶ S` and `a ∈ Γ(S,⊤)`, pulling back the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` is,
932	after the unit-pullback comparison `q = pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the
933	base-changed function `p.appTop a`:
934	`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫ q = q ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a)`.
935	Proved by transposing under the pullback-pushforward adjunction to
936	`unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm`.
937	Project-local — the single irreducible new claim underlying `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
938	lemma scalarEnd_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
939	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫
940	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
941	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
942	        scalarEnd (p.appTop a) := by
943	  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
944	    (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf) |>.injective
945	  -- `homEquiv_naturality_left` collapses the LHS transpose; the RHS transpose
946	  -- (`homEquiv_naturality_right`) is supplied as a term because positional `rw` cannot match
947	  -- the identical-printing `homEquiv` under the `X.Modules` diamond (memory
948	  -- `grquot-functor-dropped-termmode`).
949	  -- `hq` is the lemma `..._homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` restated in the `Scheme.Modules`
950	  -- adjunction form (defeq to the `SheafOfModules` form), so `rw`/`congrArg` match syntactically.
951	  have hq : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
952	        (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)
953	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p))
954	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) :=
955	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit _
956	  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, hq]
957	  refine (unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm p a).trans ?_
958	  exact (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv_naturality_right
959	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p))
960	        (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))).trans
961	      (congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))) hq)).symm
962	
963	/-- The action of `matrixEnd M` on the `j`-th free injection: `ιFree j ≫ matrixEnd M`
964	expands as the sum over rows `∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ ιFree k`. Project-local helper
965	for `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
966	lemma ιFree_matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
967	    (j : Fin d) :
968	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEnd M
969	      = ∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
970	  rw [matrixEnd, SheafOfModules.ιFree]
971	  simp only [SheafOfModules.free]
972	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom,
973	    ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_desc]
974	  rfl
975	
976	/-- **(a) Matrix endomorphism naturality under pullback** (`lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback`).
977	For `p : T ⟶ S` and `M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S,⊤)`, the pullback of the matrix
978	endomorphism `matrixEnd M` is, after the free-pullback comparison `Q = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)`,
979	the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix `p.appTop • M` (entrywise comorphism):
980	`(pullback p).map (matrixEnd M) = Q.hom ≫ matrixEnd (p.appTop.mapMatrix M) ≫ Q.inv`.
981	Reduces, on each one-element biproduct component, to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
982	Project-local. -/
983	lemma matrixEnd_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) {d : ℕ}
984	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
985	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)
986	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom ≫
987	        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) ≫
988	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv := by
989	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final p
990	  -- Reduce to the naturality square (cancel the trailing `Q.inv`).
991	  rw [← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]
992	  -- Check the two maps out of the coproduct `(pullback p).obj (free (Fin d))` agree on each
993	  -- free injection `(pullback p).map (ιFree i)` (the cofan of the preserved colimit).
994	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext
995	    (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (Scheme.Modules.pullback p) _ _
996	      (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d))) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
997	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj, Cofan.mk_pt]
998	  -- `Q.hom` is, by construction of `pullbackFreeIso`, the Mathlib free-pullback comparison.
999	  have hQhom : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
1000	      = (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) (Fin d)).hom := rfl
1001	  -- The free injection cancels against `Q.hom` into the unit-pullback comparison
1002	  -- (`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`), which is where `scalarEnd_pullback` lives.
1003	  have key : ∀ k : Fin d,
1004	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree k)
1005	          ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
1006	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
1007	            ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
1008	    intro k
1009	    rw [hQhom]
1010	    exact SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom _ k
1011	  -- LHS: `map (ιFree i) ≫ map (matrixEnd M)` collapses to `map (ιFree i ≫ matrixEnd M)`,
1012	  -- then `ιFree_matrixEnd` turns it into a row sum, distributed by additivity of the pullback.
1013	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
1014	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)),
1015	    ← Functor.map_comp]
1016	  -- `erw` (defeq matching) is needed to see `ιFree i ≫ matrixEnd M` under `(pullback p).map`.
1017	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd M i]
1018	  erw [Functor.map_sum]
1019	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
1020	  -- RHS: cancel `map (ιFree i) ≫ Q.hom` into `pullbackObjUnitToUnit ≫ ιFree i`, then expand.
1021	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
1022	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom,
1023	    key i]
1024	  erw [Category.assoc]
1025	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) i]
1026	  erw [Preadditive.comp_sum]
1027	  -- Match term by term: each entry reduces to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
1028	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => ?_)
1029	  erw [Functor.map_comp]
1030	  rw [Category.assoc, key k]
1031	  erw [reassoc_of% scalarEnd_pullback p (M k i)]
1032	  erw [Category.assoc]
1033	
1034	/-- **Triple-overlap multiplicativity of the bundle transition (C2)**
1035	(`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul`): over each triple overlap the base-change transports of the
1036	three bundle transitions satisfy `ĝ_{JK} ∘ ĝ_{IJ} = ĝ_{IK}`, in the form required by
1037	`Scheme.Modules.glue`. At the matrix level this is the Cramer-inverse cocycle
1038	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` (`lem:gr_cocycle`, `universalMinorInv`); the substantive
1039	work is transporting the three transitions to the common triple overlap via
1040	`pullbackBaseChangeTransport` and aligning endpoints with `glueData_bridge_*`.
1041	
1042	NOTE (scaffold): the body is the remaining hard step of the bundle cocycle (~50-100 LOC of
1043	matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping); the signature is the exact `_hC2` hypothesis of
1044	`Scheme.Modules.glue` instantiated at `theGlueData d r` and `bundleTransitionData`. -/
1045	theorem bundleTransition_cocycle (d r : ℕ) (I J K : (theGlueData d r).J) :
1046	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
1047	        (Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).f I K))
1048	        ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)
1049	        (bundleTransitionData d r I J) ≪≫
1050	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
1051	          (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid (theGlueData d r) I J K)).app _ ≪≫
1052	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
1053	        ((theGlueData d r).t' I J K ≫
1054	          Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f J K) ((theGlueData d r).f J I))
1055	        ((theGlueData d r).f J K) ((theGlueData d r).t J K ≫ (theGlueData d r).f K J)
1056	        (bundleTransitionData d r J K) ≪≫
1057	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
1058	          (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt (theGlueData d r) I J K)).app _
1059	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
1060	          (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src (theGlueData d r) I J K)).app _ ≪≫
1061	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
1062	        (Limits.pullback.snd ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).f I K))
1063	        ((theGlueData d r).f I K) ((theGlueData d r).t I K ≫ (theGlueData d r).f K I)
1064	        (bundleTransitionData d r I K) := by
1065	  -- Reduce the iso-level cocycle to the underlying morphism equality of free sheaves over
1066	  -- the triple overlap `V_IJK`. Each `bundleTransition I J` unfolds (via `bundleTransitionData`,
1067	  -- `bundleTransition`) to `pullbackFreeIso (f I J) ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso (M_IJ) ≪≫
1068	  -- (pullbackFreeIso (t I J ≫ f J I)).symm`, where `M_IJ = ΓSpecIso.inv.mapMatrix (X^I_J)⁻¹`.
1069	  apply Iso.ext
1070	  -- REMAINING (L3, the substantive transport — flagged for iter-062 standalone). The hom-level
1071	  -- goal is the composite of three `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`s. The proof has three parts,
1072	  -- two of which are already in hand:
1073	  --   • L2 `matrixToFreeIso_mul`  : composing two `matrixToFreeIso`s gives `matrixEnd` of the
1074	  --       matrix product (with the order reversed).  [DONE, this file]
1075	  --   • L1 `bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix` : `(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the
1076	  --       triple-overlap ring `S_I`.            [DONE, this file]
1077	  -- The MISSING ingredient is the *matrixEnd-under-pullback naturality* together with the
1078	  -- base-change bridge:
1079	  --   (a) For `p : T ⟶ S` and `M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S,⊤)`,
1080	  --         `(Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)`
1081	  --           = (pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom ≫ matrixEnd (M.map (p.appTop)) ≫
1082	  --             (pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv,
1083	  --       i.e. pulling back the matrix automorphism is, after the free-pullback comparisons,
1084	  --       the matrix automorphism of the base-changed matrix `M.map p.appTop`
1085	  --       (`p.appTop : Γ(S,⊤) ⟶ Γ(T,⊤)` the comorphism on global sections). This is where the
1086	  --       `pullbackComp` reassociations of `pullbackBaseChangeTransport` and the three
1087	  --       `glueData_bridge_*`/`pullbackCongr` casts cancel the `pullbackFreeIso` comparisons,
1088	  --       leaving each transport equal to `matrixEnd` of a base-changed Cramer inverse on the
1089	  --       common free sheaf `O_{V_IJK}^d`.
1090	  --   (b) The base-change maps `Γ(U^I_J,⊤) ⟶ Γ(V_IJK,⊤)` induced by the projections /
1091	  --       triple transition `t'` agree (through `ΓSpecIso` naturality on the affine charts and

1132	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
1133	
1134	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
1135	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
1136	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
1137	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
1138	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
1139	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
1140	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
1141	
1142	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
1143	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
1144	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
1145	unchanged. -/
1146	
1147	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
1148	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
1149	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
1150	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
1151	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
1152	  F : T.Modules
1153	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
1154	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
1155	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
1156	  epi : Epi q
1157	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
1158	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
1159	
1160	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
1161	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
1162	coincide). -/
1163	def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
1164	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
1165	
1166	lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
1167	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
1168	
1169	lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
1170	    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
1171	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
1172	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
1173	
1174	lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
1175	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
1176	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
1177	  -- term-mode (the `T.Modules` def-diamond blocks positional category `rw`)
1178	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
1179	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
1180	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
1181	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
1182	
1183	/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid on `RankQuotient r d T`. -/
1184	instance rqSetoid (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) : Setoid (RankQuotient r d T) where
1185	  r := RankQuotient.Rel
1186	  iseqv := ⟨RankQuotient.rel_refl, RankQuotient.rel_symm, RankQuotient.rel_trans⟩
1187	
1188	/-- The **pullback action** on a rank-`d` quotient: pull the target sheaf and quotient map
1189	back along `ψ`, re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via `pullbackFreeIso`. The
1190	result is again an epimorphism (pullback preserves epis) onto a rank-`d` locally free sheaf
1191	(`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`). -/
1192	noncomputable def rqPullback {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
1193	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : RankQuotient r d T' where
1194	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).obj x.F
1195	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q
1196	  epi :=
1197	    -- fully explicit: the def-diamond on `T.Modules` blocks `Epi`-instance search, so
1198	    -- `x.epi` is threaded through `map_epi`/`epi_comp` by hand
1199	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _

120	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
121	  exact RingHom.map_add (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b
122	
123	/-- `scalarEnd` of a finite sum is the sum of the `scalarEnd`s. Project-local. -/
124	lemma scalarEnd_sum {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → Γ(X, ⊤)) :
125	    scalarEnd (∑ i ∈ s, f i) = ∑ i ∈ s, scalarEnd (f i) := by
126	  classical
127	  induction s using Finset.induction with
128	  | empty => simp [scalarEnd_zero]
129	  | insert a s ha ih => rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, scalarEnd_add, ih]
